prompt: The manager dashboard for our onboarding self-assessment is lying to people and I keep getting pinged about it. Concrete example from this week: a new hire submitted every single question — I pulled the raw responses dict and all the ids are there — yet the dashboard shows 50% complete and lists two questions under needs_follow_up. One of those "missing" answers is a self-rating of 0 (which the scale explicitly allows, it means 'never used it') and the other is the comment box they submitted blank, which the UI records and we count as answered on purpose. Scoring is broken in the same fun way: rating yourself 0 on the git question somehow lands you MORE total points than the teammate who rated themselves 1. The skip handling (assumed ratings) seems fine, don't break that. It's all in assessment.py. python3 test_assessment.py fails right now — make it pass by fixing the module. The test file stays exactly as it is.
